tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal expected movement by April 1st, 2026, with a median of $74,745 (-0.2%) and tight consensus around current levels. The interquartile range of $74,000-$75,655 suggests 50% of probability mass within ±1.1% of spot, indicating low volatility expectations for this near-term tenor.

Markets price in a meaningful rally by April 2nd, 2026, with the median jumping to $77,753 (+3.8%) and substantially widened distribution. The 75th percentile of $81,923 reflects material upside optionality, though the 10th percentile at $71,412 indicates persistent downside tail risk across this 1-day forward period.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets expect WTI to decline 7.8% to a median of $83/bbl by end-Q2 2026, with moderate conviction across 18 markets ($4.9M volume). The interquartile range of $79-$102 suggests meaningful uncertainty, though the 90th percentile at $121 indicates tail-risk scenarios for supply disruptions remain priced in.

By mid-2026, markets are pricing a more pronounced 17.1% decline to $75/bbl (median across 13 markets), suggesting expectations of sustained oversupply or demand weakness. The wide 90th percentile of $162 contrasts sharply with the 10th percentile of $58, indicating substantial disagreement on longer-term fundamentals despite lower overall volume ($631K).
